rationale: Both reviews correctly identify zero current duplication/reuse violations vs Mathlib or TauCeti (confirmed by inspecting the diff's imports and all new decls: AffineAlgGroup etc. all build directly on HopfAlgebra/FiniteType/IsGeometricallyReduced/GroupLike/IsNilpotent without overlap). Review 2's sole finding (the AlgPoints signature vs. an external roadmap generalisation) is a speculative future-API concern, not a material reuse defect grounded in the shown diff (AlgPoints is defined but unused, and renaming/generalising is a contested preference no maintainer would insist on for this PR).
